{{Quote|Do not let the artifact fall into their hands. Take it. Use what they desire most against them.|Durrei|<ref name="Relic" />}}
'''Durrei''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native of [[Elrood/Legends|Elrood]] and a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. His family was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] when he was very young, and it was during their executions that Durrei first used his latent [[The Force/Legends|Force]] abilities, using the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] to kill the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]]. Following his parents' murders, Durrei committed himself to the dark side of the Force, and joined a splinter faction of the Empire in their fight against the Imperials. Durrei eventually discovered clues about a rare Force artifact called the [[Kashi Mer talisman]], and used his new contacts to pursue the relic.

==Biography==
===Early life===
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Durrei was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in [[13 BBY/Legends|13 BBY]], during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and he grew up on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Elrood/Legends|Elrood]]. When he was very young, a squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] tried to arrest [[Durrei's mother]], who they suspected of [[Treason/Legends|treason]] against the Empire. When [[Durrei's father]] resisted, the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] opened fire, destroying all in front of them. Faced with such destruction, Durrei unleashed his latent [[Force power/Legends|Force ability]], using the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] to destroy the soldiers. However, despite his efforts, he was not able to save the lives of his parents.<ref name="Relic" />

Durrei fled his home and began searching for other [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] that could teach him in any way. Driven by the [[Death/Legends|death]] of his parents, he dedicated himself to the dark side, to avoid ever being helpless again. During this period, he acquired a [[Color/Legends|red]]-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], which he soon learned to use,<ref name="Relic" /> and he became the lover of<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /> the [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark side adept]] and [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]]<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> [[Arden Lyn]].<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /> After a number of [[Standard year/Legends|years]], at some point after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="Relic" /> of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="JvS" /> Durrei was approached by the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Ennix Devian]],<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /> who persuaded the young [[darksider]] to join him in fighting against the remnants of Empire—Durrei readily agreed. Devian had stolen Imperial technology which he planned to turn against the Empire and though Durrei later learned that Devian was the leader of<ref name="Relic" /> the [[restored Empire]],<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /> an Imperial splinter faction, Durrei was content to serve him so long as other Imperials were killed.<ref name="Relic" />

===Relic hunting===
[[File:Durrei2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Durrei uses the Force to grab the relic.]]
In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], at the age of twenty-two, Durrei heard of a mysterious [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Dark side artifact/Legends|artifact]], a [[Kashi Mer talisman|relic]] of the ancient [[Kashi Mer Dynasty]]<ref name="Relic" /> that had previously been possessed by<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> his lover<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /> Arden Lyn.<ref name="EGW" /> A prism-shaped gray rock, approximately twenty by ten [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]], the relic reportedly conveyed on its possessor the ability to tap directly into the dark side. The relic had been stolen by [[Reda Jalooz]], a confused student of the Force, and had surfaced in the [[Corva sector/Legends|Corva sector]].<ref name="Relic" />

Desperate for the device, Durrei convinced Devian to give him [[Troop/Legends|troops]] and former Imperial equipment to track it down.<ref name="Relic" /> Devian had his own motivations for aiding Durrei and the warlord hoped to be able to harness the artifact's rumored power to cause [[Star/Legends|stars]] to go [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]].<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /> The relic was acquired by a [[Gotal/Legends|Gotal]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] by the name of [[Mahk'khar]]. Durrei arranged to trade with the Gotal for the relic on the abandoned world of [[Jandoon]]. In exchange for the relic, Durrei was to give over [[Meela]], the kidnapped daughter of a local professor, [[Oron]], as well as 10,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="Relic" />

Traveling in a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]], he met Mahk'khar in some of the abandoned ruins on the planet. However, the two were ambushed by a group of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[operative]]s, trying to claim the relic. Accompanied by only four [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], Durrei fought off some of the operatives before [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetically]] retrieving the relic from Mahk'khar's hands and retreating with it, leaving his bodyguards to buy him [[Time/Legends|time]]. The New Republic operatives soon after continued their pursuit of Durrei.<ref name="Relic" /> Devian later reached the conclusion that Durrei was no longer of use to him and he betrayed Durrei and Lyn to [[Antinnis Tremayne]], an Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] and former sparring partner of Devian<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /> who resented Lyn because<ref name="tep">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref> he had previously battled her.<ref name="EGW" />

==Personality and traits==
Durrei's life was dramatically affected by the murder of his parents, leading him to dedicate himself to the dark side, to avoid ever being helpless again. It also led him to being easily convinced to join the fight against the Empire. Durrei lusted after the Kashi Mer talisman, and he was not above giving over Meela, the kidnapped daughter of a local professor, in order to get it. Durrei had dark-colored [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="Relic" />

==Powers and abilities==
At an early age, Durrei showed a latent ability with the Force, unleashing it to destroy the stormtroopers who came to arrest his mother. Mostly self-taught, at some point Durrei acquired a red-bladed lightsaber, which he soon learned to use. He was able to use telekinesis.<ref name="Relic" />

==Behind the scenes==
Durrei was created by [[George R. Strayton]] and was illustrated by [[Doug Shuler]] for the article "[[Relic (article)|Relic]]," which was published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 6|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 6]] in [[May]] [[1995]].<ref name="Relic" />
